sourceMovie.title: Fanboys
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Fanboys works as a adventure movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The adventure and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Thelma & Louise
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Fanboys clicked for you, Thelma & Louise (1991) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and crime, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Seeking a Friend for the End of the World
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Fanboys clicked for you, Seeking a Friend for the End of the World (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Fanboys?
faq.answer: Try Thelma & Louise, Seeking a Friend for the End of the World, Into the Night—each shares DNA with Fanboys while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Fanboys?
faq.answer: Fanboys (2009) is best described as Adventure, Comedy, Crime, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Fanboys worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
